tasks.title: Package Structure and Embedded Files
tasks.description:
## Context
This task establishes the foundational package structure for the init-openspec-setup change. The design specifies a new `internal/init/openspec/` sub-package following the same pattern as existing packages (agentsmd, gitignore, skills). The embedding pattern mirrors `skills/embed.go` which uses `//go:embed` with `fs.Sub` to walk and extract files.

## Scope
- Create the internal/init/openspec/ sub-package directory
- Copy the littlefactory schema files into the embedded directory
- Implement the embed.go file with schema extraction logic

## OpenSpec checklist (reference)
- [ ] 1.1 Create `internal/init/openspec/` directory
- [ ] 1.2 Copy `openspec/schemas/littlefactory/` contents into `internal/init/openspec/embedded/schema/`
- [ ] 1.3 Create `internal/init/openspec/embed.go` with `//go:embed all:embedded/schema` and `ExtractSchema(projectRoot string) error` function using `fs.Sub` and `fs.WalkDir` (mirror `skills/embed.go` pattern)

The task list can be found at openspec/changes/init-openspec-setup/tasks.md

## Implementation plan (suggested chunks)
1. Create directory structure: internal/init/openspec/ and internal/init/openspec/embedded/schema/templates/
2. Copy openspec/schemas/littlefactory/schema.yaml to internal/init/openspec/embedded/schema/schema.yaml
3. Copy all files from openspec/schemas/littlefactory/templates/ to internal/init/openspec/embedded/schema/templates/
4. Create internal/init/openspec/embed.go with //go:embed all:embedded/schema directive
5. Implement ExtractSchema(projectRoot string) error using fs.Sub and fs.WalkDir to copy to openspec/schemas/littlefactory/
6. Verify the package compiles with go build ./internal/init/openspec/

## Acceptance criteria
- internal/init/openspec/ directory exists with embed.go
- embedded/schema/ contains schema.yaml and templates/ directory with all template files
- ExtractSchema function extracts files to openspec/schemas/littlefactory/ preserving directory structure
- go build ./internal/init/openspec/ succeeds
- All checklist items above are implemented and verified
- The task list at openspec/changes/init-openspec-setup/tasks.md is updated

## Key references
- proposal.md (section: What Changes)
- design.md (section: Decisions - 3. Embed schema files in the binary)
- internal/init/skills/embed.go (reference implementation for embedding pattern)

tasks.title: Core OpenSpec Setup Logic
tasks.description:
## Context
This task implements the main logic for the openspec sub-package: checking that the openspec binary is installed and setting up the schema and config. The binary check uses exec.LookPath (the standard Go approach) and must run as a precondition before any init steps. The Setup function orchestrates schema extraction and conditional config file creation.

## Scope
- Implement CheckInstalled() using exec.LookPath
- Implement Setup() that calls ExtractSchema and conditionally writes config.yaml
- Config is only created if it doesn't exist (preserve user customizations)

## OpenSpec checklist (reference)
- [ ] 2.1 Create `internal/init/openspec/openspec.go` with `CheckInstalled() error` using `exec.LookPath("openspec")`
- [ ] 2.2 Add `Setup(projectRoot string) error` function that calls `ExtractSchema` and conditionally writes `openspec/config.yaml`
- [ ] 2.3 Config logic: create `openspec/config.yaml` with `schema: littlefactory` only if file does not exist

The task list can be found at openspec/changes/init-openspec-setup/tasks.md

## Implementation plan (suggested chunks)
1. Create internal/init/openspec/openspec.go with package declaration
2. Implement CheckInstalled() error that calls exec.LookPath("openspec") and returns a clear error message if not found
3. Define defaultConfig constant with "schema: littlefactory\n"
4. Implement Setup(projectRoot string) error that: (a) creates openspec/ dir with os.MkdirAll, (b) calls ExtractSchema(projectRoot), (c) checks if openspec/config.yaml exists, (d) only writes config if missing
5. Verify with go build and manual testing

## Acceptance criteria
- CheckInstalled() returns nil when openspec is in PATH
- CheckInstalled() returns descriptive error when openspec is not in PATH
- Setup() creates openspec/ directory if missing
- Setup() extracts schema files to openspec/schemas/littlefactory/
- Setup() creates openspec/config.yaml only if it doesn't exist
- Setup() preserves existing openspec/config.yaml content
- All checklist items above are implemented and verified
- The task list at openspec/changes/init-openspec-setup/tasks.md is updated

## Key references
- design.md (section: Decisions - 2. Binary check via exec.LookPath)
- design.md (section: Decisions - 4. Config file handling)
- specs/openspec-setup/spec.md (section: OpenSpec binary prerequisite check, OpenSpec config setup)

tasks.title: Integrate into Init and Upgrade
tasks.description:
## Context
This task wires the new openspec sub-package into the existing init and upgrade orchestration. The openspec binary check must run as a precondition before step 1, and the openspec setup becomes the new step 5 in init (step 4 in upgrade). The step totals change from 4 to 5 for init and from 3 to 4 for upgrade.

## Scope
- Add openspec.CheckInstalled() call at the start of Run() and Upgrade()
- Add new setup step to both Run() and Upgrade()
- Update step counts in logger initialization
- Add appropriate log output for the new step

## OpenSpec checklist (reference)
- [ ] 3.1 Update `internal/init/init.go` `Run()` to call `openspec.CheckInstalled()` before any steps, returning error if not found
- [ ] 3.2 Add new step 5 in `Run()` calling `openspec.Setup(projectRoot)` with logger output
- [ ] 3.3 Update logger total from 4 to 5 in `Run()`
- [ ] 3.4 Update `internal/init/upgrade.go` `Upgrade()` to call `openspec.CheckInstalled()` before any steps
- [ ] 3.5 Add OpenSpec setup step to `Upgrade()` and update logger total from 3 to 4

The task list can be found at openspec/changes/init-openspec-setup/tasks.md

## Implementation plan (suggested chunks)
1. Add import for the openspec sub-package in internal/init/init.go
2. Add openspec.CheckInstalled() call at the top of Run(), before newLogger, returning error with guidance if not found
3. Change newLogger(4) to newLogger(5) in Run()
4. Add setupOpenSpec(log, projectRoot) function and call it after installSkills
5. Implement setupOpenSpec: log.Step("Setting up OpenSpec"), call openspec.Setup, log sub-operations for schema and config
6. Add import and openspec.CheckInstalled() at the top of Upgrade() in upgrade.go
7. Change newLogger(3) to newLogger(4) in Upgrade()
8. Add setupOpenSpec call after installSkills in Upgrade()
9. Verify with go build and manual test of both init and upgrade

## Acceptance criteria
- littlefactory init fails with clear error if openspec not in PATH (before creating any files)
- littlefactory init shows [1/5] through [5/5] step numbering
- Step 5 logs "Setting up OpenSpec" with sub-operations
- littlefactory upgrade also checks for openspec and runs the setup step
- littlefactory upgrade shows [1/4] through [4/4] step numbering
- All checklist items above are implemented and verified
- The task list at openspec/changes/init-openspec-setup/tasks.md is updated

## Key references
- design.md (section: Decisions - 5. Step ordering in init)
- specs/init-command/spec.md (section: Init orchestrates all setup steps)
- specs/upgrade-command/spec.md (section: Upgrade command for existing projects)
- internal/init/init.go (current Run function)
- internal/init/upgrade.go (current Upgrade function)

tasks.title: Tests
tasks.description:
## Context
This task adds comprehensive tests for the new openspec sub-package and updates existing integration tests. The codebase uses standard Go testing with table-driven tests and t.TempDir() for isolation. Existing tests in init_test.go and upgrade_test.go need updates to account for the new openspec prerequisite check and the additional step.

## Scope
- Unit tests for CheckInstalled() covering found and not-found cases
- Unit tests for ExtractSchema() verifying file extraction
- Unit tests for Setup() verifying conditional config creation
- Update existing init and upgrade integration tests

## OpenSpec checklist (reference)
- [ ] 4.1 Write tests for `CheckInstalled()` (binary found and not-found cases)
- [ ] 4.2 Write tests for `ExtractSchema()` verifying schema.yaml and template files are extracted to correct paths
- [ ] 4.3 Write tests for `Setup()` verifying config creation when missing and config preservation when existing
- [ ] 4.4 Update existing init integration tests to account for the openspec prerequisite check and new step count
- [ ] 4.5 Update existing upgrade integration tests to account for the openspec prerequisite check and new step count

The task list can be found at openspec/changes/init-openspec-setup/tasks.md

## Implementation plan (suggested chunks)
1. Create internal/init/openspec/openspec_test.go
2. Write TestCheckInstalled with subtests: binary found in PATH returns nil, binary not found returns error with descriptive message
3. Write TestExtractSchema verifying schema.yaml and all template files are created under openspec/schemas/littlefactory/
4. Write TestSetup with subtests: config created when missing (verify content), config preserved when existing (verify not overwritten)
5. Update internal/init/init_test.go to ensure openspec is in PATH (or mock), verify 5-step output format
6. Update internal/init/upgrade_test.go similarly for 4-step output format
7. Run go test ./internal/init/... to verify all tests pass

## Acceptance criteria
- All tests pass: go test ./internal/init/...
- CheckInstalled tests cover both found and not-found cases
- ExtractSchema tests verify schema.yaml and all template files exist at correct paths
- Setup tests verify config creation when missing and preservation when existing
- Existing init and upgrade integration tests still pass with updated expectations
- All checklist items above are implemented and verified
- The task list at openspec/changes/init-openspec-setup/tasks.md is updated

## Key references
- specs/openspec-setup/spec.md (all scenarios as test cases)
- specs/init-command/spec.md (modified scenarios for testing)
- specs/upgrade-command/spec.md (modified scenarios for testing)
- internal/init/init_test.go (existing tests to update)
- internal/init/upgrade_test.go (existing tests to update)
